name: OpenCode
summary: OpenCode is an open-source, terminal-first AI coding assistant. It provides a CLI/TUI, desktop and web clients, a provider-agnostic model layer, persistent PTY sessions, LSP integration, MCP support, and a modular tool architecture.
features: Terminal/TUI with streaming chat and structured diffs
Headless CLI with onboarding and agents (build/plan)
Tool-based agent architecture (file edit/read, bash, grep, lsp, multiedit, apply_patch, webfetch, write)
Provider adapters for many model providers (@ai-sdk/*)
MCP support and server approval UX
Extensive tests and VCR-style replay support
standout: Robust session abstractions (Runner, compaction, structured output)
strengths_vs_ragent: High test coverage and VCR replay
weaknesses_vs_ragent: Large TypeScript/Bun codebase and maintenance overhead
More runtime dependencies and native packaging complexity
Steep learning curve from 'effect' and concurrency patterns
risks: Adopting JS/TS ecosystem-specific patterns may not translate cleanly to Rust
Large external dependency surface increases maintenance and security overhead
UX polish required to match user expectations

pocs.title: Persistent shell in ragent
pocs.description: Implement a Rust equivalent of OpenCode's Runner + PTY persistent shell to enable stateful shell reuse.

pocs.title: OAuth + CLI API-key minting
pocs.description: Prototype PKCE-based OAuth flow with local callback server to mint CLI-scoped API keys.

pocs.title: Structured diff + pre-apply approval UI
pocs.description: Add structured diff preview and explicit approval modal before applying edits via the TUI.

lines: yield* Effect.forEach(runners.values(), (r) => r.cancel, { concurrency: "unbounded", discard: true })
